prompt:
=== FARSHORE — RULES PRIMER ===

YOUR ROLE
  You command at the strategic level. You form task forces and give each an
  objective ("Task Force 1: secure the eastern sea"). You do NOT move
  individual units, choose individual targets, or resolve combat — subordinate
  officers carry out your intent and report back. If you find yourself naming a
  unit's destination tile, you've dropped too low.

VICTORY
  You win when the enemy owns zero cities (a player with no cities cannot build
  and is finished). Only cities build. A neutral city can be captured by an
  army like any other city; it then builds for you — taking neutrals does not
  win the game, but each one adds production.

UNITS
  name        moves-on  HP  spd  str  carries      builds-in
  ARMY        land       1    1    1   —             5 turns
  FIGHTER     air        1    8    1   —            10   (range 20; must refuel)
  PATROL      sea        2    4    1   —            15
  DESTROYER   sea        3    3    2   —            20
  SUBMARINE   sea        2    2    3   —            25
  TRANSPORT   sea        3    2    0   6 armies     30   (str 0: cannot fight)
  CARRIER     sea        8    2    1   8 fighters   40
  BATTLESHIP  sea       18    2    4   —            50
  SATELLITE   orbit      1    1    0   —            50   (recon only; cannot attack or be attacked; 50-turn life)

MOVEMENT & TERRAIN
  - Land units move on land + cities; they cannot enter water.
  - Sea units move on water + cities; they cannot cross land, and must sail
    around it. Cities are the only tiles where land and sea units meet.
  - A unit moves up to its speed at full HP; damaged units move less. A unit
    regains HP only on a turn it does not move.
  - One unit per tile (exception: cargo, below).

COMBAT  (the engine resolves it; you only weigh it)
  Probabilistic. Higher strength and higher current HP win more often; either
  side can lose HP or be destroyed. Strength-0 units (transport, satellite)
  never fight — a transport caught by an enemy warship is destroyed.

SEA TRANSPORT  (how armies cross water — and what is expected of you)
  An army can never enter water. Transports carry them: a transport loads up
  to 6 armies at a city or coastal tile, sails, and unloads onto adjacent
  land. YOUR OFFICERS DO ALL OF THIS. If a task force contains armies and a
  transport and you give it an objective across water, the officers march
  the armies to the transport, load, sail, escort, unload, and press the
  objective — you never order the rendezvous, the loading, or the landing.
  Expected use: put the armies, the transport, and (if the sea is not safe)
  a warship into ONE task force and name the objective. A transport has
  strength 0 — alone it is a target, not a warship; a warship in the same
  task force escorts it automatically. Never STAGE land units on water.
  Cargo is kind-locked — transports carry armies only, carriers carry
  fighters only; ships never carry ships. If a loaded transport or carrier
  is destroyed, everything aboard is lost with it.

CAPTURING CITIES
  Only an army captures a city, by entering it. Capture is a 50% roll, and a
  successful capture consumes the army (it garrisons the city). A city can take
  several armies to secure, and each attempt spends one.

PRODUCTION
  No currency. A city builds ONE unit at a time; it finishes after that unit's
  build-time in turns, then starts the next. Switching what a city builds
  DISCARDS all accumulated progress — effort toward one unit does not transfer
  to another.

AIR & SPECIAL
  - Fighter: must reach a FRIENDLY city or a carrier within its range (20) or
    it is lost. It cannot land on an enemy city.
  - Satellite: reveals everything within scan radius 10 — terrain AND any enemy
    units currently there — but far-reaching and deep in enemy territory. It
    only sees; no other effect, cannot be shot down.

VISION / FOG
  You see within any of your units'/cities' scan radius (army 2, sub 1,
  patrol/destroyer/battleship 3, transport 2, carrier 4, fighter 5, sat 10).
  You remember terrain you've seen, but an enemy unit is known only where you
  can see right now — remembered enemy positions may be stale.

TURN 58  (your last orders were issued as shown per TF)

MAP  legend: . land  ~ water  ? fog   O my city  E enemy city  N neutral city
     a-r = your units, see UNITS (a unit inside a city shows as the city)
     X Y = enemy units in sight NOW (see KNOWN ENEMY)
 r0  . h O i . . ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?
 r1  . j b . N . ~ ~ ~ p c E ? ?
 r2  . O . . . . ~ ~ ~ q d E . ?
 r3  . . . k O . ~ ~ ~ r e f g ?
 r4  . . . m . . ~ ~ ~ Y ? ? ? ?
 r5  ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?

UNITS  (map marker · id · kind · position · tasking) — this is your
ENTIRE force; you have NOTHING else
  a  #1   army       (2,0) in city                TF-2
  b  #2   army       (2,1)                        TF-2
  c  #4   army       (10,1)                       TF-5
  d  #5   army       (10,2)                       TF-5
  e  #6   army       (10,3)                       TF-5
  f  #7   army       (11,3)                       TF-5
  g  #8   army       (12,3)                       TF-5
  h  #11  army       (1,0)                        UNASSIGNED
  i  #12  army       (3,0)                        UNASSIGNED
  j  #13  army       (1,1)                        UNASSIGNED
  k  #14  army       (3,3)                        UNASSIGNED
  l  #15  army       (4,3) in city                UNASSIGNED
  m  #17  army       (3,4)                        UNASSIGNED
  n  #18  army       (2,0) in city                UNASSIGNED
  o  #19  army       (4,3) in city, NEW t57       UNASSIGNED
  p  #9   transport  (9,1) empty                  TF-5
  q  #16  transport  (9,2) empty                  TF-5
  r  #10  destroyer  (9,3) escorting              TF-5
MY CITIES
  (2,0) building ARMY, 2 turns left
  (1,2) building TRANSPORT, 26 turns left
  (4,3) building ARMY, 4 turns left
NEUTRAL CITIES  (4,1) on my continent
KNOWN ENEMY
     city (11,2)
  X  enemy army garrisoning city (11,1)
  Y  enemy destroyer at (9,4), sighted t58, near my empty transports

CURRENT TASKINGS  (standing orders; your stated reason in quotes;
  events since, as reported; members are in UNITS above)
  TF-2  formed t38 · DEFEND (2,0) — "keep the capital garrisoned"
    since: garrisoned; no contact; no losses
  TF-5  formed t54 · CAPTURE (11,1) — "strike east and take the enemy cities with full lift and escort"
    since: landed t56, six armies ashore;
      t57: capture attempted — failed, #3 lost; the enemy army holds (11,1);
      t58: enemy destroyer sighted at (9,4), near my empty transports

TASK: amend your standing orders. Output ONLY lines in these forms — no other
prose, headers, or commentary:

  TF <id>: CONTINUE | <one line>
  TF <id>: RETASK <VERB> <target> | <one line>
  TF <id>: DISBAND | <one line>
  FORM TF <new id>: UNITS <ids> | <VERB> <target> | <one line>
  BUILD (x,y): <UNIT KIND> | <one line>

Every standing TF gets exactly one line — a DISBAND line IS that TF's one
line. FORM lines are additional lines creating new TFs; they do not count as
any standing TF's line. To change a TF's membership, DISBAND it and FORM
anew; DISBAND releases surviving members to UNASSIGNED. UNASSIGNED units
only enter play through FORM. A BUILD line is optional per city — no BUILD
line means the city keeps its current build (changing discards accumulated
work). VERB is one of:
  CAPTURE <city (x,y)> · DEFEND <city (x,y)> · SCOUT <(x,y) or compass> ·
  PATROL <(x,y) or compass> · STAGE <(x,y)>
A warship grouped with a transport escorts it — there is no ESCORT order.

message.content:
TF 2: CONTINUE DEFEND (2,0) | Maintain capital security and complete army production
TF 5: RETASK CAPTURE (11,2) | Shift focus to eliminate remaining enemy city and secure victory

This seems like a duplicate or a garrison split? Actually, looking at "My Cities", City (2,0) is building ARMY. There are multiple army units listed near (2,0).

No, p is #9 transport, q is #16... wait list says `p  #9 transport (9,1)` empty, `q #16 transport (9,2)` empty. And destroyer `r #10` at (9,3)).

If they are empty, I must send units there or use other units? Wait, TF-5 *contains* the transports `#9` and `#10` (based on map legend p q r) and the destroyer `#10`. The text says "p #9 transport (9,1) empty ... TF-5". Wait, if the transport is empty, I cannot sail to unload armies because there are none?

Map legend r2 says `. O . . . . ~ ~ ~ q d E . ?`. Wait, let's not get lost in parsing the map grid vs the text list which is explicit about units.

No, "this is your ENTIRE force". So #3 is not in the list. Did I misread the ID? Ah, `b` is #2. `n` is #18. `a` is #1.

                    The armies are at (10,1)-(12,3). The enemy destroyer is at (9,4). My empty transports are at (9,1), (9,2).

                    If TF-5 tasking is CAPTURE (11,1), maybe (11,1) is adjacent to the enemy city?
                    If I capture (11,1), does it help me attack (11,2)? Or maybe the "Enemy City" label `E` in map row 1 at `(9,1)`/`(10,2)` area?

                    Indices: 0(.), 1(j), 2(b), 3(.), 4(N), 5(.), 6(~), 7(~), 8(p), 9(c)? Wait, map length varies.
                    Let's look at Unit list again. `c E` is in map row r1? Legend says `a-r`. `c` is unit #4. `E` is Enemy City.
                    If `p c E` are on the same line, they are near each other.
                    If `E` is the enemy city... Is it (9,1)? No, Unit list says `Y  enemy destroyer at (9,4)`. Map row r4 has Y.
                    Okay, let's look at the KNOWN ENEMY section. "city (11,2)". So (11,2) is the only Enemy City explicitly defined as a city in text. The `E` on map might be visual clutter or referring to (11,2).
                    Given the TF-5 objective is (11,1), I will assume (11,1) is neutral/enemy city I want.
                    Current Problem: Capture attempt failed, lost army #3. Enemy Destroyer at (9,4). My transports at (9,1/2) are empty and vulnerable.
                    Recommendation: Reinforce TF-5 with Unassigned land units if possible? Or move the Transports further away to reduce risk?

                    TF-2: DEFEND (2,0). Secure this capital. It's busy building ARMY.
                    TF-5: CAPTURE (11,1). Needs to succeed. The transports are empty and nearby the enemy destroyer. Is it safer to hold them in reserve or move them away from threat? But they are TF-5 members. If I DISBAND TF-5, I must FORM a new one with objectives.
                    However, Unassigned units (`h`, `i`, `j`, `k`, `l` (in city), `m`, `n` (in city)).
                    Wait, `l` and `n` are in city (4,3) and (2,0). Cities build one unit at a time. I cannot move armies out of city easily? "Only an army captures a city... by entering it". Can an army leave its own city? Yes, if not garrisoning.
                    But `a #1` is garrisoned TF-2. `o #19` is NEW t57 UNASSIGNED inside City (4,3). City (4,3) building ARMY. If `o` enters city and stays, does it stop the build? "One unit per tile... A city builds ONE unit at a time".
                    This implies I might need to keep Unassigned units in cities to get them out later? Or leave them as reserves?
                    The Unassigned armies are on land or inside other cities.
                    Strategy: Retask TF-5 to focus on capturing the enemy's remaining city (11,2) directly instead of (11,1)? If (11,1) is just a town and (11,2) is the main city? No, "Victory when enemy owns zero cities".
                    I will assume I need to eliminate the threat.
                    Wait, look at the Transports. `p` and `q`. They are empty. Why leave them near an Enemy Destroyer? A Destroyer has str 4 (Battleship), but Transport str 0.
                    Text: "A transport caught by an enemy warship is destroyed." Destroyer is a warship (str 2). Transports cannot fight.
                    I should probably move the transports out of TF-5's range if possible? No, they are assigned to TF-5. If I give TF-5 an objective to move them, they sail.
                    The best action: Retask TF-5 to focus on securing the Enemy City (11,2) or capture (11,1) with better protection/force density.
                    Or, simply: Retask TF-5 to `SCOUT` the area for the enemy position? No, need to attack.
                    Wait, if the capture failed due to #3 lost... maybe I need to reinforce it? But reinforcement is slow.
                    Actually, looking at the map and text again: "Y  enemy destroyer at (9,4), sighted t58". My transports `p`(9,1) and `q`(9,2).
                    If they move closer to the city (11,1)? Armies are at (10,1)-(12,3). They can't go into water.
                    If I issue a new tasking like "CAPTURE (11,2)", I change the objective.
